equipment.name: Arisaka Type 38
equipment.notes: The primary Japanese infantry rifle of World War II, derived from an 1897 design. The long length reflected the tradition of attaching a bayonet for close combat.

equipment.name: Arisaka Type 99
equipment.notes: Replaced the Type 38 from 1939. More powerful calibre; some versions included a monopod and anti-aircraft sights. Late-war versions show significant quality decline.

equipment.name: Type 96 Light Machine Gun
equipment.notes: Japan's squad automatic weapon through 1941. Required lubricated cartridges to feed reliably.

equipment.name: Type 11 Grenade Discharger
equipment.notes: Often incorrectly called a 'knee mortar' by Allied soldiers (firing it from the knee would break the leg). Shoulder-fired or braced against the ground, it gave Japanese infantry sections organic indirect fire capability.
